chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: Today we will learn everything about strings in JavaScript.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: A string is a sequence of characters used to represent text.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: Strings can be created using single quotes, double quotes, or backticks.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The length property returns the number of characters in a string.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: Strings are immutable in JavaScript, meaning you cannot change individual characters.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The charAt method returns the character at a specified index.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The indexOf method returns the index of the first occurrence of a specified value.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The lastIndexOf method returns the index of the last occurrence of a specified value.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The slice method extracts a section of a string and returns it as a new string.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: Slice takes two parameters: the start index and the optional end index.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The substring method is similar to slice but does not accept negative indices.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The replace method replaces a specified value with another value in a string.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The replaceAll method replaces all occurrences of a specified value.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The toUpperCase method converts a string to uppercase letters.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The toLowerCase method converts a string to lowercase letters.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The trim method removes whitespace from both sides of a string.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The split method splits a string into an array of substrings based on a separator.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The includes method checks if a string contains a specified value and returns boolean.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: The startsWith and endsWith methods check if a string starts or ends with a specified value.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: String interpolation using template literals is the modern way to create dynamic strings.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: You can also use the repeat method to repeat a string a specified number of times.

chunks.title: JavaScript Strings and String Methods
chunks.text: Understanding string methods is very important as strings are used everywhere in programming.

text: Today we will learn everything about strings in JavaScript. A string is a sequence of characters used to represent text. Strings can be created using single quotes, double quotes, or backticks. The length property returns the number of characters in a string. Strings are immutable in JavaScript, meaning you cannot change individual characters. The charAt method returns the character at a specified index. The indexOf method returns the index of the first occurrence of a specified value. The lastIndexOf method returns the index of the last occurrence of a specified value. The slice method extracts a section of a string and returns it as a new string. Slice takes two parameters: the start index and the optional end index. The substring method is similar to slice but does not accept negative indices. The replace method replaces a specified value with another value in a string. The replaceAll method replaces all occurrences of a specified value. The toUpperCase method converts a string to uppercase letters. The toLowerCase method converts a string to lowercase letters. The trim method removes whitespace from both sides of a string. The split method splits a string into an array of substrings based on a separator. The includes method checks if a string contains a specified value and returns boolean. The startsWith and endsWith methods check if a string starts or ends with a specified value. String interpolation using template literals is the modern way to create dynamic strings. You can also use the repeat method to repeat a string a specified number of times. Understanding string methods is very important as strings are used everywhere in programming.
